body:
# Week 2026-W22 — Mon 25 May to Sun 31 May 2026

**Inaugural weekly entry.** Established 24 May 2026 as Goal 2 in the [[Personal/passion-fit/coaching/2026-05-24-loren-pf-restart-plan|Loren PF restart plan]] (sibling to the daily journal practice — see [[Library/processes/pf-weekly-loop]] for the process this entry follows).

No last-week feedback this week — the practice starts now. From W23 onwards the entry will roll up the previous week's daily journals + Garmin daily files + Cowork session shape.

## This week — plan

### Shape of the week (work + life context)

Bank holiday Monday 26 May (UK) but Pete is in Lanzarote so a normal work day for him. **Time-critical paying-window items at the front of the week**: DGT speeding fine (50% discount expires Mon 26), Petroleos Marinos (Jane-pressure), Hindley rents + DD amendment (effective 1 June so this week). Tue 27 the May Sygma wages BACS goes out. Thu 29 four EU Skills sub-invoices. **Active job assists on Pete's calendar**: Mon 10:00-14:00 CT VLS (Tom), Wed 13:30-17:30 PB PLS (Tom), Fri 10:00-14:00 La Carabela community (Tom). Mon evening 17:30-19:00 PF seminar (Teams). LeakGuard tiered Phase 1 build will pick up if Pete creates the Stripe account on Camello Blanco S.L.

**The non-training pressure point this week**: work cut-off discipline. Pete has named it explicitly in the Loren message — late-night work is harming sleep, the early-morning bike sessions need protected. Michaela's been asked to help by dragging him out of the office. That's the test.

### IPSATIVE goals — this week

- **Daily micro-goal theme:** *Stop working at the cut-off time*. The specific cut-off hour isn't fixed yet — surface this in tomorrow night's journal session and set it. Once set, the daily question becomes binary: did I stop, yes or no?
- **Mid-week goal (by Wed evening)**: Have completed Mon group pool swim + Wed early-morning Turbo Intervals 05:40-07:00. The Wed bike is the leading indicator — if Pete hits it, the work cut-off discipline is paying off in measurable recovery.
- **End-of-week goal (Sun 31)**:
  - 7 journal entries written, none skipped
  - This weekly entry's "Last week feedback" section populated for the W23 entry
  - Majority of planned sessions complete (target: 5 of 7 — 3 swims + 2 of 3 runs + 2 bikes is "majority")
  - Average sleep score for the week > 65 (Saturday's was 81, Friday's was 57 — the floor needs to come up)

### Behaviour focus

**Control the Controllables** is the lead behaviour this week. Pete's diagnosis on 23 May was that the prior six months had drifted because energy went to the uncontrollable (recurring fires, external pressure) at the expense of the controllable (work cut-off, sleep discipline, scheduled training). The cut-off time is a pure controllable. So is showing up to the planned swims on the planned mornings. Lean here.

### Training sessions (planned)

| Day | Session | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Mon 25 | Swim Tech: 8am Group Pool Swim (08:00-09:30) | Xhale → calendar |
| Mon 25 | Running Endurance: 3pm Short Run (15:00-16:30) | Xhale → calendar |
| Tue 26 | (Free for slippage per Pete's plan) | — |
| Wed 27 | **Turbo Intervals: 5:40am Indoor Bike** (05:40-07:00) — leading indicator | Xhale → calendar |
| Thu 28 | Swim Endurance: 9.30am Open Water with Kimbo (09:30-11:00) — distance | Xhale → calendar |
| Thu 28 | Running Endurance: 3pm Short Run (15:00-16:30) | Xhale → calendar |
| Fri 29 | **Turbo Intervals: 5:40am Indoor Bike** (05:40-07:00) | Xhale → calendar |
| Sat 30 | Swim Tech: 8am Open Water Swim (08:00-09:30) — 700m each way, focus on form | Xhale → calendar |
| Sat 30 | Running Endurance: 3pm Short Run (15:00-16:30) — pencilled | Xhale → calendar |

3 swims locked (Mon / Thu / Sat), 2 bikes locked (Wed / Fri), 3 runs pencilled (Mon / Thu / Sat). Runs explicitly treated as flex per Pete: "treat them as pencilled in and see how the week goes". Tuesday clear for slippage.

### Commitments to Loren / coach loop

- **Daily Xhale paste**: at the end of each Cowork journal session, the script produces a paste-ready Xhale summary. Goal is 7 of these into Xhale this week — same channel cadence Loren is used to.
- **One thing Pete would value Loren's view on**: how to handle the "embarrassment / pedestal" dynamic when re-engaging with the PF seminars (Mon evening). Pete owned this in the Tom Q2 response on 23 May. Bringing it up explicitly to Loren now signals he's not just naming it once and burying it.
- **Diary-sync option**: if Loren picks Option 1 (live GCal share), Pete drops the embed URL into Xhale. If Option 2, the daily-summary block gets added to the journal practice. Pending Loren's call.

## Recovery — start-of-week baseline (Garmin)

Pulled from `Personal/health/garmin/2026-05-22.md` and `Personal/health/garmin/2026-05-23.md` — last full data points before the week starts.

- **Fri 22 May**: Sleep score 57 (POOR) — 4h 56m. HRV 45 last night, status BALANCED. Training readiness 50 (MODERATE). 1 activity — open water swim 52m.
- **Sat 23 May**: Sleep score 81 (GOOD) — 8h 19m. HRV 46, status BALANCED. Training readiness 54 (MODERATE). No activities recorded.

Sleep variance of 24 points across consecutive nights — that's the inconsistency the cut-off discipline is meant to address.

Forward-looking metric source: the `garmin-daily-pull` cron writes fresh files into `Personal/health/garmin/` twice daily (07:00 + 17:00 Atlantic/Canary). Each file now carries a rich `training` block (status / ACWR / VO2 / race predictions) and `activities[]` with training-effect chips + HR-zone breakdown + sport-specific stats — so weekly rollup can compute Z2% and ACWR avg directly without re-pulling. By Sun 31 May this entry's "Last week feedback" section will be populated from those 7 files.

## Last week — feedback

*Inaugural entry — no previous data to roll up.*

## Reflection on the week

*Walked + locked 31 May at the airport ahead of the Manchester flight. Xhale block posted to Loren same morning. Lives here (not W23) per dashboard `splitWeekEntry()` convention — see [[Library/lessons/2026-05-31-pf-weekly-reflection-lives-in-closing-week-file]].*

### Goals, hit rate

> I am really pleased with the week. I don't think it could have gone better. Yes mistakes have been made, but they have actually been needed to be made.

*Plan-of-record was the W22 plan Pete wrote on Sunday 24 May and posted to Xhale (this entry above). Delivered in full: 3 swims, 3 runs, 2 bikes, all sessions Pete had written into the plan. Sleep average 73.7 against the over-65 target. 6 daily journals plus this weekly entry. The named miss was the Wednesday 05:40 turbo time-slot, recovered as a 17:17 ride.*

### Commitment Continuum, the week's shape

> The plan had to force me at the beginning but then I got more motivated. The discipline built the motivation.

*Mon and Tue Compliant. Wed dipped after the late Leakbusters spiral. Thu to Sat Committed, climbing toward Compelled by Saturday.*

### Ownership

> The Wednesday morning bike thing is a classic example. I would have just focussed on the morning, whereas the process with Claude got me to realise the failure was before then. This learning then built into the week.

*Cross-link: [[Personal/passion-fit/journal/2026-05-27]] where the upstream-cause framing first landed.*

### Control the Controllables

> The work cut off is still challenging, still needs work, but it's now done consciously and with thought.

*5 of 6 working days held the cut-off. Wed evening was the one slip. Saturday sign-off about 18:00 was clean (the late Claude poke was a brief return, not working-late).*

### HF Matrix, the week's position

Clean climb across the week into HIGH FUNCTIONING by Thursday, held Friday and Saturday.

### Reflecting and the bigger picture

> The key thing for me this week is that I have to think about the bigger picture. Sometimes when I feedback, my feedback is based on the moment, not stepping back and looking at things as a whole and actually asking, is there something else that could have been caught earlier? The Wednesday morning bike thing is the classic example again. I would have just focussed on the morning, but stepping back showed the failure was hours earlier in the evening. Going through this process every day is embedding it into my head. During the daytime I am now questioning myself more.

### One lesson for the coming week

> Most failures this week weren't the missed thing, they were the earlier moment I called a want-to-do a should-do. Prioritising properly means catching that earlier, at the moment of choosing, not on the way home. The failure point is upstream of where it shows.

### Xhale proactivity

> Another big win for me this week is how proactive I have been with Xhale. As you know, I had fallen into bad habits for weeks with not uploading promptly. This caused missed opportunities in terms of you helping me, and in my own recall later. Every session this week was done promptly.

### How the practice felt this week

> The new process with Claude is working. It might seem like overkill and techno-geek stuff, but it's working. It's already provided huge benefit this week that I would have missed. It's working because it challenges me and asks questions, it knows my data inside out, no hiding from it, makes me sit and go through the process and think about it, no shortcuts.
>
> The short sessions you have pushed, i.e. 30 minute runs, have really worked.

### Body observation

Sleep held strong all week (average 73.7, range 65 to 79). HRV trended softer (average 41 against 47 last week), a hard week's training showing in the recovery numbers.

### Dashboard

> Speaking of overkill, I built an automated dashboard by backdooring into Garmin. It helps me see all my info how I want to see it, links in all the journal entries, and keeps everything in one place for me. Here's the week view for the week we are feeding back on:
>
> https://pete-health-dashboard.vercel.app/week/2026-W22

## Loren's feedback

*(Will be appended when Pete brings comments back from Xhale.)*
